commands.review_summary:
The PR updates the SSH and OpenShell remote-directory preflight scripts to validate path components without overwriting caller positional arguments, and adds regression tests for appended commands seeing the original target/root args.

PR surface: Source -4, Tests +56. Total +52 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The linked issue includes a current-main shell repro showing $1/$2 rewritten to path components, and source inspection confirms appended SSH/OpenShell commands depend on those args staying intact.

target.title: Fix SSH sandbox remote directory args
target.body:
Fixes openclaw/openclaw#93344

AI-assisted: yes. I understand the code changes in this PR; local Codex/autoreview was not run because the maintainer explicitly asked not to run autoreview yet.

## Summary

- Preserve the caller-supplied `$1`/`$2` values while the SSH remote-directory preflight validates path components.
- Apply the same positional-argument preservation to the OpenShell sibling preflight.
- Add regression coverage proving appended shell commands still see the original target/root arguments after validation.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sandbox/ssh.test.ts extensions/openshell/src/openshell-core.test.ts`
  - Passed on rebased branch `b566c7464e`: 2 Vitest shards, 49 tests.
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
  - Passed.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Direct SSH sandbox and OpenShell remote-directory preflights used `set --` while validating path components, so appended upload/cleanup commands could receive rewritten positional parameters instead of the validated caller-supplied target/root paths.

Real environment tested: Popcorn OpenClaw with `luna-native` configured for `sandbox.backend=ssh`, `mode=all`, `workspaceAccess=rw`; the live proof ran on the same patch before the final rebase, installed as `2026.6.8-beta.1 (1d9717f)`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a non-delivering `openclaw agent --agent luna-native ... --json` proof turn that instructed the agent to use the sandbox `exec` tool to print `pwd`/`hostname`, write marker `OPENCLAW_SSH_FIX_1D9717F`, read it back, remove it, and report cleanup status.

Evidence after fix: Run id `bcf6248d-3731-4686-85d4-4c45aa561eb9`; the result reported exactly one sandbox `exec` tool call, with sanitized config confirming SSH sandbox mode and workspace RW access.

Observed result after fix: The command ran in the SSH sandbox workspace on `sandbox-luna`, read back marker `OPENCLAW_SSH_FIX_1D9717F`, and reported `cleanup: succeeded`.

What was not tested: I attempted to rebuild/redeploy the live package after rebasing the final branch to `b566c7464e`, but current `upstream/main` packaging is blocked by an unrelated parse error in `src/commands/configure.shared.ts` where `password` is exported twice. I did not run autoreview.
target.author: dmorn
